Description: Commit 93ac319 narrows metadata-only review-close cleanup by checking whether the existing `closing_commit_sha` points at a tracker-only backfill before deleting it, but `commitIsMetadataOnlyTrackerBackfill` treats any `git show` failure as metadata-only (`return true`). Reproduced against the checked-out source by building `/tmp/ddx-review` from `cli/`, running it in a temp directory with `DDX_BEAD_DIR` and no git repo, creating a review bead, setting `closing_commit_sha=1234567890abcdef1234567890abcdef12345678`, and then closing the bead. The bead closes successfully but the stored `closing_commit_sha` disappears. That regresses FEAT-019 replay/audit semantics for portable/manual review workflows: when git metadata is unavailable, a valid previously recorded implementation boundary is erased even though the command cannot prove it was a tracker-only backfill.
Suggested fix: Make `commitIsMetadataOnlyTrackerBackfill` fail open for provenance preservation when commit inspection is unavailable, or otherwise only clear an existing `closing_commit_sha` after positively proving the referenced commit is a `.ddx/beads.jsonl`-only backfill; add regression coverage for closing a review bead with a pre-existing full SHA in a non-git workspace.","id":"hx-2a146683","issue_type":"task","labels":["helix","phase:build","review-finding","severity:medium","area:cli","area:bead"],"owner":"erik","priority":0,"spec-id":"cli/cmd/bead.go","status":"closed","title":"correctness: preserve existing review provenance when git lookup is unavailable","updated_at":"2026-04-10T11:00:59.253241422Z"}

File: cli/cmd/install.go:244
Category: integration
Severity: medium
Description: Registry installs explicitly create `.ddx/plugins/<name>` as a project-local symlink when the manifest root target is global (`~/...`) so project-relative plugin paths still resolve. The new manifest-driven local install path does not mirror that behavior: it creates the global symlink only. Repro: a local plugin whose manifest sets `install.root.target: ~/.ddx/plugins/sample-plugin` produces `global_plugin_exists=yes`, `project_plugin_exists=no`, and still installs skills. Any code or docs that resolve plugin resources through `.ddx/plugins/<name>/...` now break in local developer installs.
Suggested fix: When `installLocal` honors a global root target, also create the same project-local `.ddx/plugins/<name>` symlink that `InstallPackage` creates for registry installs.","id":"hx-3f3c0e74","issue_type":"task","labels":["helix","phase:build","review-finding","severity:medium"],"owner":"erik","priority":0,"spec-id":"cli/cmd/install.go","status":"closed","title":"integration: keep project-local plugin path for local installs with global roots","updated_at":"2026-04-10T04:33:09.938605658Z"}

File: docs/helix/02-design/solution-designs/SD-015-agent-routing-and-catalog-resolution.md:175
Category: correctness
Severity: medium
Description: The new minimal-routing schema records `RoutingOutcome`, `QuotaSnapshot`, and `BurnSummary` only by `harness` and `surface`. Earlier in the same design, however, each `CandidatePlan` carries `CanonicalTarget`, `ConcreteModel`, `EstimatedCostUSD`, and `PerformanceMetrics` because routing decisions can differ materially by resolved model or exact pin. A single surface can host very different models with different latency, reliability, and cost characteristics. If DDx aggregates outcome and burn data only at the harness/surface level, runs for one model will contaminate routing signals for another, making model-specific scoring and exact-pin behavior incorrect.
Suggested fix: Extend the minimal routing-metrics schema with a model-identifying dimension such as `canonical_target` or `concrete_model` (or explicitly define an equivalent normalization key), and update FEAT-014/SD-015 so performance, cost, and burn inputs remain attributable to the resolved model rather than only the surface.","id":"hx-4a12a619","issue_type":"task","labels":["helix","phase:build","review-finding","severity:medium","area:agent","area:docs"],"owner":"erik","priority":0,"spec-id":"docs/helix/02-design/solution-designs/SD-015-agent-routing-and-catalog-resolution.md","status":"closed","title":"correctness: keep routing metrics model-specific","updated_at":"2026-04-10T03:47:24.607997607Z"}

Category: integration
Severity: medium
Description: AutoCommit now changes the git working directory to filepath.Dir(filePath) but still runs git add with the original filePath. That works for absolute paths like .ddx/beads.jsonl in bead close, but it breaks existing relative nested-path callers. For example, from /Users/erik/Projects/ddx, running `cd cli && git add -n cli/cmd/bead.go` fails with `fatal: pathspec 'cli/cmd/bead.go' did not match any files`, because the path is interpreted relative to the new cwd as cli/cli/cmd/bead.go. cli/cmd/doc.go passes relative document paths into AutoCommit, so doc stamping auto-commit will now fail for nested docs paths.\nSuggested fix: Normalize filePath before staging when AutoCommit changes directories. Description: Commit 34b87b9 repoints ddx-1210706d.closing_commit_sha to 9653820049db7edebe0374431544b1b8a8dbae88 because that SHA is a pure tracker-only commit. But FEAT-019 replay uses closing_commit_sha as the original commit boundary: it checks out the parent of that SHA and compares against that commit's diff. File: docs/helix/02-design/solution-designs/SD-016-process-metrics.md:191
Category: correctness
Severity: high
Description: SD-016 defines `cost_state=known` whenever a session row reports `cost_usd` and says bead lifecycle cost should use `session.cost_usd` whenever it is present. But FEAT-014 and FEAT-006 explicitly define `cost_usd=-1` as the sentinel for unknown cost, not a real price. Implementing SD-016 as written would treat unknown-cost sessions as known values, producing negative dollar totals and skipping the estimation/unknown fallback for a core FEAT-016 metric.
Suggested fix: Update SD-016 so `cost_usd=-1` is mapped to `cost_state=unknown` and excluded from direct cost sums, with estimation or unknown fallback applied only to non-sentinel values.","id":"hx-72b9a368","issue_type":"task","labels":["helix","phase:build","review-finding","severity:high"],"owner":"erik","priority":0,"spec-id":"docs/helix/02-design/solution-designs/SD-016-process-metrics.md","status":"closed","title":"correctness: treat cost_usd=-1 as unknown in SD-016","updated_at":"2026-04-10T00:21:23.11757511Z"}

Description: During this last-commit review, the supported `ddx bead close hx-010a50cf` flow closed a metadata-only `action:review` bead and persisted `closing_commit_sha=d20a24fd8ff635481f19c934e8643472ed0a7cde` on the new tracker row. That SHA is the previously reviewed provenance-repair commit, not a close commit created by the review workflow, and FEAT-019 explicitly says tracker-only backfills should omit `closing_commit_sha` rather than point replay at an unrelated metadata or earlier implementation boundary. The repo source and tests still indicate review beads should suppress this field, so the durable tracker state produced by the current supported CLI path remains misleading.
Suggested fix: Make the supported bead-close path omit `closing_commit_sha` for metadata-only review closes when no landed close commit exists, add regression coverage for that no-new-commit path, and repair hx-010a50cf so its closing provenance is absent rather than pointing at d20a24f.","id":"hx-73a3c3c0","issue_type":"task","labels":["helix","phase:build","review-finding","severity:medium","area:bead","area:cli"],"owner":"erik","priority":0,"spec-id":".ddx/beads.jsonl","status":"closed","title":"correctness: metadata-only review close still stamps prior HEAD provenance","updated_at":"2026-04-10T10:25:57.586690085Z"}

Category: integration
Severity: medium
Description: The new close flow writes bead status first, auto-commits .ddx/beads.jsonl, and only then writes closing_commit_sha back to the store. In a clean temp git repo with git.auto_commit=always and DDX_BEAD_DIR unset, running 'ddx bead close <id>' leaves 'git status --short' dirty with ' M .ddx/beads.jsonl'. The landed HEAD commit 9494afc82408cac9a650d616fc3207197e1dfbd6 contains only the status=open->closed change, while the durable closing_commit_sha appears only in the uncommitted worktree diff. That means the command still fails to land close provenance atomically and leaves replay/audit metadata outside the recorded close commit.
Suggested fix: Restructure bead close so the recorded closing_commit_sha is present in the same landed commit that closes the bead, or perform and persist a second deterministic commit/update so git status is clean after close and HEAD contains the final provenance-bearing row.","id":"hx-73c95bab","issue_type":"task","labels":["helix","phase:build","review-finding","severity:medium"],"owner":"erik","parent":"hx-df089a3d","priority":0,"spec-id":"cli/cmd/bead.go","status":"closed","title":"integration: persist close provenance in the landed tracker commit","updated_at":"2026-04-09T23:42:54.706314959Z"}

Description: `checkInstalledPlugins` iterates every row in installed state without filtering by package type, even though `InstalledEntry` records resource installs as well as plugins. File: .github/workflows/ci.yml:156
Category: drift
Severity: medium
Description: The new frontend-e2e job sets up Node, keys its cache from `cli/internal/server/frontend/package-lock.json`, and runs `npm ci`, `npx playwright install`, and `npm run test:e2e`. The active `typescript-bun` concern and TP-002 declare Bun as the frontend package manager and script runner, with `bun.lock` as the authoritative lockfile and `bunx`/`bun run` as the expected commands. Wiring this job to npm cements a second dependency-resolution path in CI that can drift from the declared Bun toolchain and from local/frontend build behavior.
Suggested fix: Switch the job to `oven-sh/setup-bun`, cache/install from `cli/internal/server/frontend/bun.lock`, and run the Playwright install/tests with `bunx` and `bun run` so CI matches the active concern contract.","id":"hx-76c04de5","issue_type":"task","labels":["helix","phase:build","review-finding","severity:medium"],"owner":"erik","priority":0,"spec-id":".github/workflows/ci.yml","status":"closed","title":"drift: run embedded frontend CI with Bun practices","updated_at":"2026-04-10T13:16:54.193462894Z"}

Category: integration

Description: The new compatibility merge uses one global `migrationCutoff` taken from the earliest routing outcome in the log directory and then drops every legacy session row at or after that timestamp before merging (`aggregateUsageFromRoutingMetrics` + `aggregateUsageAggs`). That still under-reports mixed stores when routing metrics exist for one harness but another harness still has legacy-only rows after that cutoff. Repro with the current binary: create `routing-outcomes.jsonl` containing only a claude row at `2026-04-09T10:00:00Z`, create `sessions.jsonl` containing that mirrored claude row plus a codex legacy row at `2026-04-10T10:00:00Z`, then run `ddx agent usage --since 2026-04-01 --format json`; the output only includes claude and silently drops codex even though the log directory still contains a valid post-cutoff legacy session.
Suggested fix: Make the compatibility boundary and dedupe key harness-scoped or row-scoped rather than global, so legacy rows without a matching routing outcome remain visible until that harness/session has actually migrated; add regression coverage for a mixed directory where one harness has routing outcomes and another still only has legacy sessions after the first outcome timestamp.","id":"hx-80c953b2","issue_type":"task","labels":["helix","phase:build","review-finding","severity:medium","area:agent","area:cli"],"owner":"erik","priority":0,"spec-id":"cli/cmd/agent_usage.go","status":"closed","title":"integration: keep legacy-only harness rows visible during mixed usage migration","updated_at":"2026-04-10T10:35:14.704961333Z"}

Category: correctness
Severity: medium
Description: Commit fb9bdf1 closes ddx-7e88c022 with closing_commit_sha=4563fa7, an abbreviated SHA. The close store documents this field as the exact closing commit SHA when known, but manual close evidence is persisted verbatim. Replay later resolves closing_commit_sha through git rev-parse to choose the baseline commit, so storing a short SHA weakens audit and replay determinism as the repository grows and abbreviations can become ambiguous or invalid. In the current tracker, this reviewed row is one of only two abbreviated closing_commit_sha values; the durable convention elsewhere is full 40-character SHAs.
Suggested fix: Normalize ddx bead close --commit input through git rev-parse before storing it, repair ddx-7e88c022 to the full 40-character 4563fa77d88cfb0f242925a927e913841b43868c value, and add regression coverage for manual close provenance normalization.","id":"hx-83ed9550","issue_type":"task","labels":["helix","phase:build","review-finding","severity:medium"],"owner":"erik","priority":0,"spec-id":".ddx/beads.jsonl","status":"closed","title":"correctness: normalize closing_commit_sha to full git SHAs","updated_at":"2026-04-10T01:54:27.646433594Z"}

Category: correctness
Severity: medium
Description: Commit f645daf fixes the stale-review-provenance case by deleting any existing `closing_commit_sha` before a review bead is closed without `--commit`, but the new branch cannot distinguish stale provenance from a valid implementation boundary that was intentionally set earlier. Reproducing against the checked-out source with `/tmp/ddx-review` built from `cli/` and `git.auto_commit: never` shows that creating a review bead, setting `closing_commit_sha` to the real HEAD via `bead update --set`, and then running `bead close` removes the field entirely from the closed bead. That regresses FEAT-019 replay/audit semantics for review beads that already carried correct provenance, because the supported close path now erases valid history rather than only suppressing misleading tracker-only backfills.
Suggested fix: Narrow the pre-close cleanup to only stale tracker-only provenance, or preserve an existing `closing_commit_sha` unless the command can prove it points at the prior metadata-only HEAD being repaired; add regression coverage for the preserve-valid-provenance path under metadata-only review close.","id":"hx-939516c7","issue_type":"task","labels":["helix","phase:build","review-finding","severity:medium","area:bead","area:cli"],"owner":"erik","priority":0,"spec-id":"cli/cmd/bead.go","status":"closed","title":"correctness: preserve valid review closing provenance on metadata-only close","updated_at":"2026-04-10T10:51:40.697890368Z"}

File: cli/internal/agent/agent_runner.go:169
Category: integration
Severity: medium
Description: The migration switched the embedded runtime to github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/agent, but the default preset in AgentRunConfig still uses `"forge"`, and the checked-in sample config still sets `.ddx/config.yaml` to `preset: forge`. In v0.1.0, the agent library only exposes presets `agent`, `benchmark`, `minimal`, `claude`, `codex`, and `cursor`; unknown names silently fall back to `agent`. That means DDx now ignores the configured/default preset name with no warning, which makes prompt behavior implicit and leaves the runtime/config surface out of sync with the migrated library.

File: cli/cmd/agent_usage.go:201
Category: integration
Severity: medium
Description: Commit 5349a98 removes the per-harness cutoff and now suppresses legacy sessions.jsonl rows only when usageMirrorKey finds a matching native_session_id, trace_id, or span_id (aggregateUsageAggs lines 201-207 and aggregateUsageFromRoutingMetrics lines 246-270). Current external-harness writes do not guarantee any of those identifiers: both the session row and routing outcome copy result.AgentSessionID, then only fill correlation IDs when present (cli/internal/agent/runner.go:722-753, cli/internal/agent/routing_metrics.go:231-256). For normal codex/claude-style runs without correlation metadata, both records remain unkeyed, so ddx agent usage now counts the same live run twice. Reproduced with the shipped CLI by creating one codex sessions.jsonl row and one matching routing-outcomes.jsonl row with identical tokens/cost but no mirror IDs; ddx agent usage --since 2026-04-01 --format json reported sessions=2, input_tokens=400, output_tokens=40, cost_usd=5 instead of one session.
Suggested fix: Preserve a compatibility boundary for unkeyed current rows, or emit a stable mirror identifier for every routing outcome/session pair before relying solely on identity-match dedupe; add regression coverage for a mixed current-format session/outcome pair that lacks native_session_id, trace_id, and span_id.","id":"hx-a30d162c","issue_type":"task","labels":["helix","phase:build","review-finding","severity:medium","area:agent","area:cli"],"owner":"erik","priority":0,"spec-id":"cli/cmd/agent_usage.go","status":"closed","title":"integration: avoid double-counting current sessions without mirror IDs in agent usage","updated_at":"2026-04-10T13:47:33.514964363Z"}

File: cli/cmd/bead.go:567
Category: correctness
Severity: medium
Description: Commit 1bc26181bae1e88cd6c29fc11f20c5904ed2ae8a normalizes `ddx bead close --commit` through `git rev-parse` before persisting the value. That fixes short SHAs inside a git repo, but it also makes manual close provenance unusable in non-git workspaces where bead commands otherwise still work. Reproduced with the built CLI in a temp directory outside git: `ddx bead create ...` succeeds, then `ddx bead close <id> --commit 1234567890abcdef1234567890abcdef12345678` now fails with `git rev-parse ... exit status 128`. Before this change, the command accepted an exact commit SHA verbatim. That is a behavior regression for portable/manual tracker workflows and contradicts the command contract that `--commit` records the exact closing commit SHA when known.
Suggested fix: Normalize only when the supplied value is an abbreviated ref in a git repository, but preserve already-full commit SHAs verbatim when git resolution is unavailable; add regression coverage for `bead close --commit <40-char-sha>` in a non-git workspace.","id":"hx-a77820e4","issue_type":"task","labels":["helix","phase:build","review-finding","severity:medium"],"owner":"erik","priority":0,"spec-id":"cli/cmd/bead.go","status":"closed","title":"correctness: preserve manual close provenance in non-git workspaces","updated_at":"2026-04-10T05:38:51.46880358Z"}

Description: Commit 58d6229309445ce3e571b85e14f1ba423b2198b1 still treats an explicit session row with cost_usd=0 as estimated whenever the model has non-zero pricing. Both sessionCostState and sessionDerivedCost continue to special-case only sess.CostUSD > 0, so a priced-model row such as model=gpt-5.4 with cost_usd=0 falls through to sessionEstimatedCost and is reported as cost_state=estimated with a non-zero derived cost instead of preserving the recorded zero. The new regression test only covers qwen/qwen3-coder-30b, whose estimator also returns 0, so it misses the case that actually matters for explicit zero-cost rows on priced models.

Pass 1 correctness: API-001 duplicates post-run and land/preserve ownership around Unified interface for dispatching work to AI coding agents.

Supports multiple harnesses (codex, claude, gemini, etc.) with output capture,
token tracking, session logging, and multi-agent quorum.

Examples:
  ddx agent run --harness codex --prompt task.md
  ddx agent run --quorum majority --harnesses codex,claude --prompt task.md
  ddx agent list
  ddx agent capabilities codex
  ddx agent doctor
  ddx agent log

Usage:
  ddx agent [flags]
  ddx agent [command]

Available Commands:
  benchmark    Run a benchmark suite comparing agent harnesses
  capabilities Show agent model and reasoning-level capabilities
  condense     Filter raw agent output to progress-relevant lines
  doctor       Check agent harness health
  list         List available agent harnesses
  log          Show agent session history
  replay       Replay a bead with a different agent harness or model
  run          Invoke an agent with a prompt
  usage        Show per-harness token and cost summary

Flags:
  -h, --help   help for agent

Global Flags:
      --config string              config file (default is $HOME/.ddx.yml)
      --library-base-path string   override path for DDx library location
  -v, --verbose                    verbose output

Use "ddx agent [command] --help" for more information about a command., and it validates candidate readiness before resolving the governing base snapshot that FEAT-006 says must control execution-document evaluation.
Pass 2 integration: API-001 uses the generic validator to choose the best candidate even though HELIX owns eligibility policy and SD-004 already defines deterministic ready ordering, leaving queue selection undefined.
Pass 3 concern-aware quality: skipped because the reviewed change is documentation-only design work with no new runtime attack surface or toolchain drift.
Pass 4 operational learnings: no AGENTS.md change required; no learnings bead required.

Description: During this review, `ddx bead close hx-01c0012d` closed a metadata-only review bead without creating a new git commit, yet the resulting tracker row recorded `closing_commit_sha=ff9a6ce2f6db77c670adcf2874b3f86278dba4e8`. That SHA is the previously reviewed hx-registry-lookpath provenance-repair commit, not the review-bead lifecycle change itself. FEAT-019 explicitly says tracker-only backfills must omit `closing_commit_sha` instead of pointing replay at an unrelated metadata or earlier implementation commit, so the underlying close-path behavior still lets review/repair beads persist misleading replay provenance even after ff9a6ce repaired one specific bead row.
Suggested fix: Make `ddx bead close` omit `closing_commit_sha` when no new close commit is actually created or when the operation is metadata-only tracker maintenance, add regression coverage for that path, and repair hx-01c0012d so its closing provenance is absent rather than pointing at ff9a6ce.","id":"hx-ae141d75","issue_type":"task","labels":["helix","phase:build","review-finding","severity:medium"],"owner":"erik","priority":0,"spec-id":"cli/cmd/bead.go","status":"closed","title":"correctness: omit misleading closing provenance for metadata-only review closes","updated_at":"2026-04-10T06:05:14.662412287Z"}

Description: Commit 8eaf934b6d106b9f513fb76bb10f2091e67b87d5 improves ddx-83777abd by repointing its closing provenance at the actual implementation commit, but it stores only the 7-character prefix `d84aa2b` instead of the exact commit SHA. If the target branch has advanced since the base revision, the ff-merge fails and the result is preserved under a hidden ref instead of being rebased and landed.

## Fix required
Before attempting ff-merge, rebase the worktree commits onto the current branch HEAD. If the rebase succeeds, then ff-merge and keep linear history. .ddx/beads.jsonl:1549:{"acceptance":"After execute-bead completes, dirty worktree changes are either restored via git stash pop or the user is explicitly warned with the stash ref so they can restore manually","archived_at":"2026-05-02T04:37:57Z","claimed-at":"2026-04-09T19:15:50Z","claimed-machine":"eitri","claimed-pid":"1385203","closing_commit_sha":"ada469a58561d9795471b0b02d10aa5c8a7f94aa","created_at":"2026-04-09T13:00:00Z","description":"execute-bead stashes dirty worktree changes but never restores them after execution.

## Current behavior
When execute-bead detects a dirty worktree, it calls git stash to checkpoint changes but never calls git stash pop. User's in-progress work remains silently stashed.

## Fix required

File: docs/helix/02-design/contracts/API-001-execute-bead-supervisor-contract.md:67
Category: correctness
Severity: high
Description: The updated contract still says the supervisor claims the bead, creates an isolated execution worktree, runs `ddx agent execute-bead` inside that worktree, and then removes the worktree. FEAT-006 defines `ddx agent execute-bead` itself as the workflow that creates the isolated worktree (step 4) and always removes it after preserve/land handling (step 12), and the shipped command implementation already does exactly that in `cli/cmd/agent_execute_bead.go:261-274`. As written, the first shipped supervisor cannot follow API-001 without layering a second worktree lifecycle around one execute-bead attempt or introducing an undocumented lower-level mode. That leaves the core ownership boundary ambiguous and makes the contract non-executable for the intended wrapper design.
Suggested fix: Make one layer own the worktree lifecycle. Either change API-001 so the supervisor invokes `ddx agent execute-bead` from the project root and only reacts to its result, or introduce and document a lower-level execute-bead primitive that accepts an already-created worktree and update FEAT-006/API-001 together.","id":"hx-f9928f75","issue_type":"task","labels":["helix","phase:build","review-finding","severity:high","area:api","area:agent","area:docs"],"owner":"erik","priority":0,"spec-id":"docs/helix/02-design/contracts/API-001-execute-bead-supervisor-contract.md","status":"closed","title":"correctness: avoid duplicating execute-bead worktree ownership in API-001","updated_at":"2026-04-10T12:45:39.684926309Z"}

Description: When a local plugin manifest sets install.root.target under ~/.ddx/plugins/<name>, installLocal now unconditionally removes .ddx/plugins/<name> in the current project before creating the compatibility symlink. That bypasses the command's existing force guard and silently deletes any pre-existing project-local plugin checkout or files even when --force was not provided.
Evidence: Reproduced by creating an existing worktree directory at .ddx/plugins/sample-plugin with a sentinel file, then running ddx install sample-plugin --local <path> for a manifest rooted at ~/.ddx/plugins/sample-plugin. The command returned success, sentinel_exists=false, and the directory was replaced by a symlink.

File: cli/cmd/bead.go:403
Category: correctness
Severity: medium
Description: Commit c38d7bbb2cf47baed8197c27cac19cb4adcd8644 documents ddx bead update <id> --set closing_commit_sha=<sha> as the supported provenance-repair path and adds a happy-path test for closed beads, but the underlying bead update implementation still routes closing_commit_sha through the generic --set handler without any normalization or verification. Unlike bead close --commit, which resolves the value through git rev-parse --verify <sha>^{commit}, bead update accepts arbitrary strings such as not-a-sha and persists them to closed beads. That leaves FEAT-019 replay and audit provenance one bad repair away from silently storing an invalid or ambiguous commit boundary even though the docs now present this as the safe replacement for hand-editing storage.
Suggested fix: Teach ddx bead update --set closing_commit_sha=... to use the same commit-resolution path as bead close --commit and reject invalid or non-commit values, then add a regression test proving invalid repair SHAs fail and valid abbreviated SHAs are normalized to full commits.","id":"hx-fd99632b","issue_type":"task","labels":["helix","phase:build","review-finding","severity:medium","area:cli","area:bead"],"owner":"erik","priority":0,"spec-id":"cli/cmd/bead.go","status":"closed","title":"correctness: validate closing_commit_sha repairs before persisting","updated_at":"2026-04-10T03:02:53.028428892Z"}
.ddx/beads.jsonl:1563:{"acceptance":"ddx bead show hx-7c184555 --json omits closing_commit_sha, and ddx bead show hx-6855d237 --json no longer reports closing_commit_sha=89d7b1193c0d8c8f980e955576da6a7545f36b04 while remaining open until that repair lands or closing only after its acceptance is satisfied","archived_at":"2026-05-02T04:37:57Z","claimed-at":"2026-04-10T08:05:04Z","claimed-machine":"eitri","claimed-pid":"1745691","created_at":"2026-04-10T05:09:17.457955974Z","description":"Review finding from fresh-eyes review.
File: .ddx/beads.jsonl:86
Category: correctness
Severity: medium
Description: Commit f58e708dc6672763e7adb31e294fc26d4da1b7db appends hx-6855d237 as a closed review-finding bead, but its acceptance requires ddx bead show hx-7c184555 --json to omit closing_commit_sha and that condition is still false after the commit. The reviewed diff only adds the hx-6855d237 row; it does not repair hx-7c184555, and ddx bead show hx-7c184555 --json still reports closing_commit_sha=2361670c68103160987c9851851819317a3b357e. The newly added row also records closing_commit_sha=89d7b1193c0d8c8f980e955576da6a7545f36b04, so replay/audit now shows hx-6855d237 as complete even though the governed repair never landed.
Suggested fix: Reopen or otherwise repair hx-6855d237 through supported ddx bead commands, clear hx-7c184555.closing_commit_sha as its acceptance requires, and only close hx-6855d237 after the tracker state actually satisfies that acceptance with non-misleading provenance.","id":"hx-fee1dec1","issue_type":"task","labels":["helix","phase:build","review-finding","severity:medium","area:bead","area:docs"],"owner":"erik","priority":0,"spec-id":".ddx/beads.jsonl","status":"closed","title":"correctness: do not close hx-6855d237 before its acceptance is true","updated_at":"2026-04-10T08:05:45.769103783Z"}
.ddx/beads.jsonl:1564:{"acceptance":"ddx bead show hx-7599316a --json reports closing_commit_sha=a6f7e9d3c43e1fc0705ea8e3077a4e4b25d3a58c, and git show --name-only --format=oneline a6f7e9d3c43e1fc0705ea8e3077a4e4b25d3a58c includes docs/helix/03-test/test-plans/TP-002-server-web-ui.md and .ddx/beads.jsonl","archived_at":"2026-05-02T04:37:57Z","claimed-at":"2026-04-10T07:53:23Z","claimed-machine":"eitri","claimed-pid":"1699844","created_at":"2026-04-10T05:24:11.148478178Z","description":"Review finding from fresh-eyes review.
File: .ddx/beads.jsonl:204
Category: correctness
Severity: medium
Description: Commit a6f7e9d3c43e1fc0705ea8e3077a4e4b25d3a58c correctly updates TP-002 to mark TC-010 as planned future coverage, but the same commit adds bead hx-7599316a with closing_commit_sha=040008c8471a99885313e5c0e7cb387de7c70196. That SHA is the prior HEAD and does not include the TP-002 fix that actually resolved the finding. FEAT-019 replay and audit flows use closing_commit_sha as the implementation boundary, so this review-finding bead now points at unrelated provenance even though the durable tracker row claims the finding is closed.
Suggested fix: Repair hx-7599316a so closing_commit_sha points at a6f7e9d3c43e1fc0705ea8e3077a4e4b25d3a58c, and keep the close/repair workflow aligned so beads closed by a landing commit record that landing commit instead of the previous HEAD.","id":"hx-ffb390a4","issue_type":"task","labels":["helix","phase:build","review-finding","severity:medium","area:bead","area:docs"],"owner":"erik","priority":0,"spec-id":".ddx/beads.jsonl","status":"closed","title":"correctness: repair hx-7599316a closing provenance to the actual fix commit","updated_at":"2026-04-10T08:31:10.345874764Z"}
.ddx/beads.jsonl:1565:{"acceptance":"Registry.Discover() uses the same LookPath abstraction as Runner.resolveHarness; a test with injected LookPath controls both routing and discovery results","archived_at":"2026-05-02T04:37:57Z","claimed-at":"2026-04-10T01:34:29Z","claimed-machine":"eitri","claimed-pid":"3930774","closing_commit_sha":"fb667a6c6ecb1e6dd8a44c65964dcb9d474aff41","created_at":"2026-04-09T13:00:00Z","description":"<context-digest>
Review area: agent harness discovery. Evidence covers Registry.Discover LookPath injection alignment with Runner.
</context-digest>
Registry.Discover() calls exec.LookPath directly instead of using the Runner's injected LookPath function.

## Impact
Tests that inject a custom LookPath cannot fully control Discover() behavior. Any future LookPath customization (caching, PATH filtering) won't apply to discovery.

## Fix required
Add a LookPath field to Registry (or pass it via constructor) so Discover() uses the same abstraction as Runner.","id":"hx-registry-lookpath","issue_type":"bug","labels":["helix","area:agent"],"owner":"erik","parent":"hx-epic-ddx-agent-migration","priority":0,"spec-id":"FEAT-006","status":"closed","title":"Wire LookPath injection through Registry.Discover","updated_at":"2026-04-10T01:39:04.66086581Z"}

File: .ddx/beads.jsonl:77
Category: correctness
Severity: medium
Description: Commit a043cfa52fc867964fc63062d26e510a090babfb closes review-finding bead hx-3fafe2df with closing_commit_sha=25786b96c7e27a478d5982f5776c7e9c89308b2f. The referenced commit is itself a tracker-only provenance repair that changes only .ddx/beads.jsonl, so FEAT-019 replay should not treat it as a governed implementation boundary. The close-path suppression in cli/cmd/bead.go only classifies labels action:review, kind:review, and phase:review as metadata-only review work, which lets phase:build review-finding beads record misleading replay provenance even when their implementation is a pure tracker backfill.
Suggested fix: Clear hx-3fafe2df.closing_commit_sha and broaden the close-path metadata-only review classification and regression coverage so tracker-only review-finding beads omit closing provenance by default.","id":"hx-e2a0e56e","issue_type":"task","labels":["helix","phase:build","review-finding","severity:medium","area:bead","area:docs","area:cli","area:agent"],"owner":"erik","priority":0,"spec-id":".ddx/beads.jsonl","status":"closed","title":"correctness: omit closing provenance on tracker-only review-finding closes","updated_at":"2026-04-10T13:09:00.023068841Z"}

{"acceptance":"ddx bead show hx-3aab36e9 --json omits closing_commit_sha, and cd cli && go test ./cmd -run TestBeadCommandsCloseOmitsMetadataOnlyReviewFindingClosingCommitSha -count=1 passes","archived_at":"2026-05-02T04:37:57Z","created_at":"2026-04-10T10:47:37.255711305Z","description":"Review finding from fresh-eyes review.
File: .ddx/beads.jsonl:80
Category: correctness
Severity: medium
Description: Commit d51f18c93d88901d833c9cc82ab280611e7690d2 correctly repairs hx-2ab700af so its closing_commit_sha now points at the actual implementation commit 58d6229309445ce3e571b85e14f1ba423b2198b1. But the same tracker state still leaves the governing review-finding bead hx-3aab36e9 closed with closing_commit_sha=9f96a3e7dc5abbc59fb6296e9d87ab9e6ecf539c, which is the unrelated agent-usage commit 'hx-80c953b2 scope usage cutoff by harness'. FEAT-019 says tracker-only backfills must omit closing_commit_sha rather than preserving an unrelated replay boundary, and cli/cmd/bead.go only clears stale provenance for labels action:review, kind:review, or phase:review. File: docs/helix/02-design/contracts/API-001-execute-bead-supervisor-contract.md:59
Category: drift
Severity: medium
Description: API-001 says the supervisor should read the ready bead set and choose the best candidate using the generic execution-ready validator. But the same contract says HELIX owns which beads are eligible for the loop, and SD-004 already defines deterministic ready ordering as priority then stable snapshot order. The generic DDx validator can say whether one bead is structurally executable; it does not define queue priority or workflow policy. Leaving candidate choice as 'best' via the validator either invents hidden DDx scheduling policy or makes the first worker's queue behavior undefined.
Suggested fix: Separate queue ordering from readiness validation. {"acceptance":"execute-bead rebases the worktree result onto current HEAD before attempting ff-merge; when the rebase succeeds the result lands without a merge commit; when the rebase conflicts the iteration is preserved under the hidden ref and the target branch remains unchanged; and tests cover the case where the target branch advanced during execution","archived_at":"2026-05-02T04:37:57Z","claimed-at":"2026-04-10T02:28:56Z","claimed-machine":"eitri","claimed-pid":"4071725","closing_commit_sha":"65134ca7caedd43b6dab6e567d001b36fb32b665","created_at":"2026-04-09T13:00:00Z","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"hx-exec-bead-stash-fix","depends_on_id":"hx-exec-bead-stash-fix","type":"blocks"}],"description":"FEAT-006 step 10 specifies 'land by rebase + fast-forward semantics' but the implementation only attempts `git merge --ff-only`.

## Current behavior
If the target branch has advanced since the base revision, the ff-merge fails and the result is preserved under a hidden ref instead of being rebased and landed.

## Fix required
Before attempting ff-merge, rebase the worktree commits onto the current branch HEAD. If the rebase succeeds, then ff-merge and keep linear history. If the rebase fails because of conflicts, preserve the iteration under the hidden ref, leave the target branch untouched, and report the non-landed result deterministically.","id":"hx-exec-bead-rebase-ff","issue_type":"bug","labels":["helix","area:agent"],"owner":"erik","parent":"hx-epic-ddx-agent-migration","priority":0,"status":"closed","title":"Implement rebase + ff semantics for execute-bead land flow","updated_at":"2026-04-10T02:31:23.760412906Z"}
{"acceptance":"After execute-bead completes, dirty worktree changes are either restored via git stash pop or the user is explicitly warned with the stash ref so they can restore manually","archived_at":"2026-05-02T04:37:57Z","claimed-at":"2026-04-09T19:15:50Z","claimed-machine":"eitri","claimed-pid":"1385203","closing_commit_sha":"ada469a58561d9795471b0b02d10aa5c8a7f94aa","created_at":"2026-04-09T13:00:00Z","description":"execute-bead stashes dirty worktree changes but never restores them after execution.

## Current behavior
When execute-bead detects a dirty worktree, it calls git stash to checkpoint changes but never calls git stash pop. User's in-progress work remains silently stashed.

## Fix required

{"acceptance":"API-001, FEAT-006, and the execute-bead command agree on a single owner for execution worktree creation and cleanup; the documented supervisor flow no longer requires nested or duplicated worktree management around one execute-bead attempt","archived_at":"2026-05-02T04:37:57Z","claimed-at":"2026-04-10T12:38:53Z","claimed-machine":"eitri","claimed-pid":"2540533","closing_commit_sha":"f577b91b349d66a9352bfd3d1332a5af82b3274f","created_at":"2026-04-10T12:12:41.773899952Z","description":"Review finding from fresh-eyes review.
File: docs/helix/02-design/contracts/API-001-execute-bead-supervisor-contract.md:67
Category: correctness
Severity: high
Description: The updated contract still says the supervisor claims the bead, creates an isolated execution worktree, runs `ddx agent execute-bead` inside that worktree, and then removes the worktree. FEAT-006 defines `ddx agent execute-bead` itself as the workflow that creates the isolated worktree (step 4) and always removes it after preserve/land handling (step 12), and the shipped command implementation already does exactly that in `cli/cmd/agent_execute_bead.go:261-274`. As written, the first shipped supervisor cannot follow API-001 without layering a second worktree lifecycle around one execute-bead attempt or introducing an undocumented lower-level mode. That leaves the core ownership boundary ambiguous and makes the contract non-executable for the intended wrapper design.
Suggested fix: Make one layer own the worktree lifecycle. Either change API-001 so the supervisor invokes `ddx agent execute-bead` from the project root and only reacts to its result, or introduce and document a lower-level execute-bead primitive that accepts an already-created worktree and update FEAT-006/API-001 together.","id":"hx-f9928f75","issue_type":"task","labels":["helix","phase:build","review-finding","severity:high","area:api","area:agent","area:docs"],"owner":"erik","priority":0,"spec-id":"docs/helix/02-design/contracts/API-001-execute-bead-supervisor-contract.md","status":"closed","title":"correctness: avoid duplicating execute-bead worktree ownership in API-001","updated_at":"2026-04-10T12:45:39.684926309Z"}

Description: When a local plugin manifest sets install.root.target under ~/.ddx/plugins/<name>, installLocal now unconditionally removes .ddx/plugins/<name> in the current project before creating the compatibility symlink. That bypasses the command's existing force guard and silently deletes any pre-existing project-local plugin checkout or files even when --force was not provided.
Evidence: Reproduced by creating an existing worktree directory at .ddx/plugins/sample-plugin with a sentinel file, then running ddx install sample-plugin --local <path> for a manifest rooted at ~/.ddx/plugins/sample-plugin. The command returned success, sentinel_exists=false, and the directory was replaced by a symlink.
Suggested fix: Apply the same collision policy used for the declared root target to the project-local compatibility path: only replace an existing entry when it is already a symlink or when --force is set, and add a regression test covering the non-force collision case.","id":"hx-fa0ffd40","issue_type":"task","labels":["helix","phase:build","review-finding","severity:high"],"owner":"erik","priority":0,"spec-id":"cli/cmd/install.go","status":"closed","title":"correctness: preserve existing project plugin dir unless --force is set","updated_at":"2026-04-10T09:09:01.361893198Z"}

File: cli/cmd/bead.go:403
Category: correctness
Severity: medium
Description: Commit c38d7bbb2cf47baed8197c27cac19cb4adcd8644 documents ddx bead update <id> --set closing_commit_sha=<sha> as the supported provenance-repair path and adds a happy-path test for closed beads, but the underlying bead update implementation still routes closing_commit_sha through the generic --set handler without any normalization or verification. Unlike bead close --commit, which resolves the value through git rev-parse --verify <sha>^{commit}, bead update accepts arbitrary strings such as not-a-sha and persists them to closed beads. That leaves FEAT-019 replay and audit provenance one bad repair away from silently storing an invalid or ambiguous commit boundary even though the docs now present this as the safe replacement for hand-editing storage.
Suggested fix: Teach ddx bead update --set closing_commit_sha=... to use the same commit-resolution path as bead close --commit and reject invalid or non-commit values, then add a regression test proving invalid repair SHAs fail and valid abbreviated SHAs are normalized to full commits.","id":"hx-fd99632b","issue_type":"task","labels":["helix","phase:build","review-finding","severity:medium","area:cli","area:bead"],"owner":"erik","priority":0,"spec-id":"cli/cmd/bead.go","status":"closed","title":"correctness: validate closing_commit_sha repairs before persisting","updated_at":"2026-04-10T03:02:53.028428892Z"}

Registry.Discover() calls exec.LookPath directly instead of using the Runner's injected LookPath function.

## Impact
Tests that inject a custom LookPath cannot fully control Discover() behavior. Any future LookPath customization (caching, PATH filtering) won't apply to discovery.

## Fix required
Add a LookPath field to Registry (or pass it via constructor) so Discover() uses the same abstraction as Runner.","id":"hx-registry-lookpath","issue_type":"bug","labels":["helix","area:agent"],"owner":"erik","parent":"hx-epic-ddx-agent-migration","priority":0,"spec-id":"FEAT-006","status":"closed","title":"Wire LookPath injection through Registry.Discover","updated_at":"2026-04-10T01:39:04.66086581Z"}

artifact: .ddx/executions/20260425T181621-f0cb3b0d/reviewer-stream.log		post-merge review: REQUEST_CHANGES
**cli/cmd/agent_execute_bead.go:119** — `rcfg, _ := config.LoadAndResolve(projectRoot, ...)` silently discards the error. This is the production dispatch path; a malformed `.ddx/config.yaml` should surface, not be swallowed into a zero-ish `rcfg`. The other two SD-024 dispatch sites (CLI `ddx work`, server `runWorker`) handle this error — execute-bead should match. Wrap with a project-relative context message and return.
**AC #2** — commit explicitly notes go-test pre-commit hook was skipped. The "pre-existing unrelated failure" claim is plausible but unverified in this review (TestBeadReviewCollapseGolden is in `cli/cmd/`, the same package this bead modifies). Either: (a) confirm the golden failure reproduces on the parent commit `696e6279` and document that in the result, or (b) regenerate the golden and include it in the bead.
**MirrorCfg removal** — the diff drops `MirrorCfg: loadExecutionsMirrorConfig(projectRoot)` from the construction. SD-024 Stage 3 calls for wiring `ExecutionsConfig.Mirror` *through the resolved-config path*. Verify `ExecuteBeadWithConfig` now sources the mirror config from `rcfg` (not from the runtime struct and not via a re-load); if it doesn't, this is a behavior regression for users who set `executions.mirror` in config.

ddx-d7aca866 is a rollup/tracking epic for ADR-022 rev 5. Its acceptance criteria are not directly implementable in a single agent attempt: - "All 9 implementation children closed (steps 1-9 from the roadmap)" - "Coordination bead 10 closed (C7 conflict resolved)" - "FEAT-006 reflects rev 5" - "Acceptance bead's soak passed for ≥1 week"  ← calendar-time gate - "CHANGELOG entry merged" Closure is gated on (a) each child bead being independently executed and closed, and (b) a ≥1-week soak window that cannot be compressed by an agent. The epic itself has no code/test deliverable; it is a rollup whose status should flip to closed only after the last child closes and the soak window elapses. Current child inventory (queried via `ddx bead list --json | jq 'parent==epic'`): - ddx-2892f2cf  open   step 1: server ingestion endpoints (register + event + backfill) - ddx-b6ab9c0b  open   step 8: FEAT-006 amendment — replace rev 3 worker-contract with rev 5 The epic description says "The 9 implementation beads + 1 coordination bead are children of this epic," but only 2 of the 10 are filed. Steps 2–7, step 9, and the coordination bead (step 10 / C7 resolution) have not been created. Filing those slices is a triage decision (scope, ordering, owners) that should be done by a human or a dedicated decomposition pass against the ADR roadmap — not auto-generated mid-execution loop, because the ADR-022 rev 5 roadmap sections name specific contracts (auth, schema, retry, ack semantics) that warrant an explicit triage review before bead text is committed. Recommended next action for the human triager: 1. Read docs/helix/02-design/adr/ADR-022-worker-client-server-architecture.md (commit 484b9a08) "Implementation roadmap" section. 2. Create beads for steps 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, 7, 9 and the coordination bead (step 10 / C7), each as a child of ddx-d7aca866 via `ddx bead dep add`. 3. Drain the resulting child queue normally; this epic will be closable once they all close and the soak/CHANGELOG ACs are satisfied. No verification_command is provided because none of {child closure, 1-week soak, CHANGELOG merge} can be checked by a 60s shell command from this worktree. The epic should remain open for human triage.	no_changes
rationale: status: needs_investigation
reason: |
  ddx-d7aca866 is a rollup/tracking epic for ADR-022 rev 5. Its acceptance criteria
  are not directly implementable in a single agent attempt:

    - "All 9 implementation children closed (steps 1-9 from the roadmap)"
    - "Coordination bead 10 closed (C7 conflict resolved)"
    - "FEAT-006 reflects rev 5"
    - "Acceptance bead's soak passed for ≥1 week"  ← calendar-time gate
    - "CHANGELOG entry merged"

  Closure is gated on (a) each child bead being independently executed and closed,
  and (b) a ≥1-week soak window that cannot be compressed by an agent. The epic
  itself has no code/test deliverable; it is a rollup whose status should flip to
  closed only after the last child closes and the soak window elapses.

  Current child inventory (queried via `ddx bead list --json | jq 'parent==epic'`):
    - ddx-2892f2cf  open   step 1: server ingestion endpoints (register + event + backfill)
    - ddx-b6ab9c0b  open   step 8: FEAT-006 amendment — replace rev 3 worker-contract with rev 5

  The epic description says "The 9 implementation beads + 1 coordination bead are
  children of this epic," but only 2 of the 10 are filed. Steps 2–7, step 9, and
  the coordination bead (step 10 / C7 resolution) have not been created. Filing
  those slices is a triage decision (scope, ordering, owners) that should be
  done by a human or a dedicated decomposition pass against the ADR roadmap —
  not auto-generated mid-execution loop, because the ADR-022 rev 5 roadmap
  sections name specific contracts (auth, schema, retry, ack semantics) that
  warrant an explicit triage review before bead text is committed.

  Recommended next action for the human triager:
    1. Read docs/helix/02-design/adr/ADR-022-worker-client-server-architecture.md
       (commit 484b9a08) "Implementation roadmap" section.
    2. Create beads for steps 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, 7, 9 and the coordination bead
       (step 10 / C7), each as a child of ddx-d7aca866 via `ddx bead dep add`.
    3. Drain the resulting child queue normally; this epic will be closable
       once they all close and the soak/CHANGELOG ACs are satisfied.

  No verification_command is provided because none of {child closure, 1-week
  soak, CHANGELOG merge} can be checked by a 60s shell command from this
  worktree. The epic should remain open for human triage.

claims"), both reachable from main via merge d1797559.

Evidence in cli/internal/server/frontend/src/routes/nodes/[nodeId]/projects/[projectId]/runs/+page.svelte:

  AC1 — Re-queue button visible on try and run rows:
    Lines 398-425: Actions cell renders "Start worker" only when
    layer === 'work'; the {:else} branch renders "Re-queue" with
    data-run-action="requeue" for try and run rows.

  AC2 — Modal prefilled with original config; idempotency key generated
        client-side:
    Lines 30-35: newIdempotencyKey() uses crypto.randomUUID() in-browser.
    Lines 37-45: openRequeueDialog() prefills run id, layer override
    (= run.layer), and a freshly generated idempotency key.
    Lines 503-551: Modal fields render readonly run id, layer override
    select, and editable+regeneratable idempotency key input.
    Lines 74-91: confirmAction() sends RUN_REQUEUE_MUTATION with the
    client-generated key.

  AC3 — work-layer "Start worker" action prefilled with original drain
        config:
    Lines 47-68: openStartWorkerDialog() pretty-prints run.queueInputs
    JSON (the original drain's queue inputs) into actionWorkerArgs.
    Lines 399-411: layer === 'work' rows render the
    data-run-action="start-worker" button.
    Lines 552-577: Modal renders source run id and prefilled
    JSON-editable Worker args textarea.
    Lines 92-114: confirmAction() validates JSON and dispatches via
    WORKER_DISPATCH_MUTATION with kind="execute-loop".

commit 12d0b618).

Implementation history (both commits are ancestors of HEAD):

- 3503f1f0 fix(agent): lock tracker-commit to prevent .git/index.lock race [ddx-da11a34a]
    Adds withTrackerLock + per-OS process-alive helpers and wraps the
    tracker-commit pair in cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go (now at
    lines ~1035-1071) so concurrent `ddx work --local` workers serialize
    git index operations on the primary .git.
- ba6882a4 fix(agent): expose tracker-lock retry/backoff policy [ddx-da11a34a]
    Adds LockRetryPolicy + DefaultLockRetryPolicy and the matching
    TestTrackerCommit_RetryBackoffPolicy suite.

Acceptance criteria mapping (verified against current worktree):

1. Tracker-commit pair wrapped in process-shared lock
   -> cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go:1039 withTrackerLock(...) wraps
      both `git add` and `git commit -m msg` calls.
2. Lockfile at .ddx/.git-tracker.lock
   -> cli/internal/agent/tracker_lock.go:94 trackerLockPath returns
      filepath.Join(projectRoot, ".ddx", ".git-tracker.lock").
3. Lock primitive reuses bead/lock_unix.go + lock_windows.go pattern
   -> cli/internal/agent/tracker_lock_unix.go and tracker_lock_windows.go
      mirror the bead-store lock shape (Mkdir-based atomic lockdir,
      pid + acquired_at sidecar files, signal(0)/Windows process probe
      for staleness).
4. Lock-error retry policy owned here (not duplicated in triage)
   -> LockRetryPolicy struct + DefaultLockRetryPolicy + step() in
      tracker_lock.go:24-78. Docstring at tracker_lock.go:12-23 records
      the contract and points triage at ActionRetryWithBackoff.
5. Aligns with TD-031 §8.5 LockContentionContract; no persisted status
   change on contention; bd/br ADR-004 compatibility preserved.
   -> No status mutation on the contention path; only an error is
      returned, which one layer up maps to execution_failed via the
      existing populateWorkerStatus path. JSONL schema is untouched.
6. Tests present and green:
   -> TestTrackerCommit_ConcurrentSafety  (tracker_lock_test.go:54)
   -> TestTrackerCommit_StaleLockRecovery (tracker_lock_test.go:115)
   -> TestTrackerCommit_RetryBackoffPolicy (tracker_lock_test.go:160)

information is rendered in execution detail view; test passes in CI") is
satisfied by the cost-tier branch of the disjunction.

what is done:
  - Read cli/internal/bead/chaos_test.go end-to-end (5 chaos tests:
    ConcurrentAppendSafety, AtomicStatusTransitions,
    ConcurrentCloseAndAppend, ConcurrentCloseNotLost,
    JSONLRoundTripIntegrity).
  - Inspected the current Backend interface in
    cli/internal/bead/backend.go: still the legacy 4-method shape
    (Init / ReadAll / WriteAll / WithLock) with constants only for
    BackendJSONL, BackendBD, BackendBR. There is no BackendAxon
    constant and no axon package under cli/internal/bead/.
  - Inspected dependency status in .ddx/beads.jsonl:
      ddx-6bb51a52 (TD: design axon-backend integration)         closed
      ddx-bbdd7564 (Introduce selectable bead Backend interface) OPEN
      ddx-95ec5ed5 (Implement axon Backend for bead tracker)     OPEN
      ddx-c9ef6c4f (Implement axon backend behind backend flag)  closed
        (closed via no_changes_rationale on attempt
        20260503T024421-4ede954b — i.e. the implementation bead
        itself reported blocked_by_dependency and did NOT actually
        implement the backend).
  - Confirmed the chaos tests use the unparameterized
    newTestStore(t) helper, which today returns the JSONL-backed
    Store from store.go. There is no axon Store, no axon Backend
    registration, and no axon test harness in this repo.

what is blocking:
  This bead's acceptance criteria require running the chaos suite
  *against the axon backend* and asserting multi-worker fairness,
  claim correctness under contention, and crash/recovery semantics.
  None of that is verifiable today because:

  1. The axon Backend implementation (ddx-95ec5ed5) is open. There
     is no axon/ subpackage, no generated gRPC client under
     cli/internal/bead/axon/pb/, and no factory entry that maps
     "axon" to a Store. Parameterizing chaos_test.go on a backend
     that does not exist would either (a) fail at construction
     time, or (b) require introducing a stub backend that the AC
     would not accept.

  2. The selectable Backend interface refactor (ddx-bbdd7564) is
     also open. TD-030 (docs/helix/02-design/technical-designs/
     TD-030-axon-bead-backend.md §"Backend interface (Go)")
     explicitly says the current 4-method interface is too coarse
     for axon and must be replaced with a per-entity surface
     (Get / Create / Update with OCC Version, AppendEvent,
     AddDep / RemoveDep, Ready, Close, Export / Import,
     ErrVersionMismatch). Until that refactor lands, there is no
     interface boundary to parameterize the chaos tests against:
     newTestStore(t) returns a concrete *Store, not a Backend, and
     the chaos tests call Store-only methods (Create, Update, Close,
     Get, ReadAll) that are not yet on the Backend contract.

  3. AC #3 ("multi-worker contention scenario verified — no
     double-claim, no lost-update") implies a claim API that does
     not exist on the JSONL Store either; it is part of the TD-030
     redesign (per-entity AppendEvent + OCC Version). Writing the
     claim-contention scenario now would have to invent an API that
     the upstream beads will define.

  4. AC #4 ("CI runs the suite for both backends") requires a
     running axon-server (per TD-030 §D1 / §D4 the axon backend
     refuses all ops when axon is unreachable). No such server is
     available in this execution environment, and there is no
     vendored test harness or "skip if AXON_ADDR unset" gate yet.

  Implementing this bead now would require either:
    (a) reproducing the axon backend, the interface refactor, the
        gRPC client generation, and a test harness in a single
        execute-bead pass — far outside the scope this bead was
        sized for, and explicitly the work of three other beads;
    (b) committing red or stub code (parameterized harness
        targeting a non-existent backend) — which the execute-bead
        contract forbids ("Do not commit partial, exploratory, or
        red code hoping the reviewer will accept it").

what a follow-up attempt would need:
  - ddx-bbdd7564 closes with the TD-030-shaped per-entity Backend
    interface, and chaos_test.go's newTestStore is refactored to
    return that Backend (or a thin adapter exposing the methods the
    chaos tests need: Create, Update, Close, Get, ReadAll, plus the
    new claim/append-event surface AC #3 references).
  - ddx-95ec5ed5 closes with a real axon Backend registered in
    cli/internal/bead/registry.go, plus a test harness AC #4 can
    rely on (either a docker-compose axon-server entry, a
    `go test`-managed server-under-test, or an explicit
    "skip if AXON_ADDR unset" gate consistent with the project's
    test-tier conventions).
  - A prerequisite bead (not yet filed, as far as I can see) for
    the axon gRPC client generation: vendor axon.proto with sha256
    pin, wire `make gen-axon-client`, commit the generated tree
    under cli/internal/bead/axon/pb/.
  - Then this bead becomes focused: parameterize newTestStore on a
    backend factory (table-driven sub-tests for "jsonl" and "axon"
    using the harness above), add the multi-worker claim/contention
    scenario the original chaos suite does not yet cover (AC #3),
    add a CI matrix entry that runs the suite against both
    backends (AC #4), and fix any axon-side issues that the new
    contention scenario surfaces.

  Recommendation for the queue: enforce the dep graph in `ddx bead
  ready` so this bead does not become eligible until ddx-bbdd7564
  AND ddx-95ec5ed5 are both closed. The textual "Depends on …"
  language in the description is not enough; a concrete
  `ddx bead dep add ddx-dfb1c6d7 ddx-95ec5ed5` (and same for
  ddx-bbdd7564) would prevent further premature dispatches that
  burn cost on no_changes_rationale outcomes.

what a follow-up attempt would need:
  - ddx-bbdd7564 closes with the TD-030-shaped per-entity Backend
    interface, and chaos_test.go's newTestStore is refactored to
    return that Backend (or a thin adapter exposing the methods the
    chaos tests need: Create, Update, Close, Get, ReadAll, plus the
    new claim/append-event surface AC #3 references).
  - ddx-95ec5ed5 closes with a real axon Backend registered in
    cli/internal/bead/registry.go, plus a test harness AC #4 can
    rely on (either a docker-compose axon-server entry, a
    `go test`-managed server-under-test, or an explicit
    "skip if AXON_ADDR unset" gate consistent with the project's
    test-tier conventions).
  - A prerequisite bead (not yet filed, as far as I can see) for
    the axon gRPC client generation: vendor axon.proto with sha256
    pin, wire `make gen-axon-client`, commit the generated tree
    under cli/internal/bead/axon/pb/.
  - Then this bead becomes focused: parameterize newTestStore on a
    backend factory (table-driven sub-tests for "jsonl" and "axon"
    using the harness above), add the multi-worker claim/contention
    scenario the original chaos suite does not yet cover (AC #3),
    add a CI matrix entry that runs the suite against both
    backends (AC #4), and fix any axon-side issues that the new
    contention scenario surfaces.

  Recommendation for the queue: enforce the dep graph in `ddx bead
  ready` so this bead does not become eligible until ddx-bbdd7564
  AND ddx-95ec5ed5 are both closed. The textual "Depends on …"
  language in the description is not enough; a concrete
  `ddx bead dep add ddx-dfb1c6d7 ddx-95ec5ed5` (and same for
  ddx-bbdd7564) would prevent further premature dispatches that
  burn cost on no_changes_rationale outcomes.

base_rev=3f9eb5ad57f1c11ee8a5aa044e4f0f0a72444c02	Opus review of 6910c2e.

EXECUTIONEVIDENCE: resolver_sub_exec.go:39-90 polls r.ExecLogs.GetExecLog(runID) every 500ms. Adapter at graphql_adapters.go:13-16 delegates to ddxexec.NewStore(workingDir).Log(runID) reading real run bundle. When available, splits by newlines, emits each as ExecutionEvent with seq-based EventID, then closes channel. ONE-SHOT dump (waits for run completion, then streams all lines at once) — not live tail. Acceptable given schema name is "executionEvidence" (final artifact), not "executionTail". Limitation worth documenting upstream.

COORDINATORMETRICS: resolver_sub_exec.go:96-150 polls CoordMetrics.GetCoordinatorMetrics(projectRoot) every MetricsPollInterval (default 1s). Adapter at graphql_adapters.go:24-37 calls reg.MetricsFor(projectRoot) on real workers.LandCoordinators registry. Diff with prev via snap != last, emits only on change. 1s interval appropriate for land events (seconds-to-minutes timescale).

BOTH TESTS REAL: httptest.NewServer + full graphql-transport-ws handshake. Coordinator test uses atomic.Pointer[CoordinatorMetricsSnap] flipped mid-test to simulate registry updates — verifies diff emission across snap1 -> snap2.

GRAPHQL_ADAPTERS.GO: proper import-cycle workaround. graphql package defines ExecLogProvider/CoordinatorMetricsProvider interfaces + CoordinatorMetricsSnap DTO; outer server package provides concrete implementations (execLogAdapter, coordMetricsAdapter). Standard pattern for nested resolver wiring.

MINOR (non-blocking):
1. executionEvidence polls forever until ctx cancels if run never completes — correct but worth docs
2. 3 separate test helpers (newGraphQLHandlerWithBeadBus/ExecLogs/CoordMetrics) duplicate ~90 lines of setup — cosmetic dedupe opportunity (note in refactor bead)

{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01B6AbPTsfUEXdAmLmB1gcKk","type":"tool_result","content":"---\nddx:\n  id: SD-015-trace\n  depends_on:\n    - SD-015\n    - FEAT-006\n---\n# Resolution Path Trace: CLI Flag → HTTP Request\n\n**Companion doc to** [SD-015 Agent Routing and Catalog Resolution](SD-015-agent-routing-and-catalog-resolution.md) and [SD-023 Agent Routing Visibility](SD-023-agent-routing-visibility.md).\n\nSD-015 and SD-023 describe *intent* — what the routing layer should do across\nfive routing modes. This document describes *reality* — how one concrete CLI\ninvocation flows through every layer of the code today, with file:line\ncitations. It exists so a newcomer debugging a routing bug can read one\ndocument instead of reverse-engineering the flow from `routing.go`,\n`discovery.go`, `agent_runner.go`, `runner.go`, and `agent_cmd.go`.\n\n**Author's invocation used throughout this trace:**\n\n```bash\nddx work --once --local --provider vidar-omlx --model-ref qwen/qwen3.6\n```\n\n**Citations are anchored to base revision `654b4cdcd9200fa8db6c38d6af9dad2acfbaa779` (2026-04-18).**\nLine numbers drift as the code evolves — see the *CI citation check* section\nat the end for how this document is kept in sync.\n\n## Current Gaps (as of 2026-04-18)\n\nEach gap links to its tracking bead. Update this table when a bead closes or a\nnew structural defect is discovered.\n\n| # | Gap | Visible at | Tracking bead |\n|---|-----|------------|---------------|\n| 1 | `RouteRequest` has no `Provider` field; `--provider` is silently dropped during normalization unless it names a registered harness. | `cli/internal/agent/types.go:337` (struct def); `cli/internal/agent/routing.go:305` (only consumed as harness alias) | [ddx-8610020e](#) — routing: add Provider field to RouteRequest |\n| 2 | `RunOptions.Provider` is a parallel path consumed by `resolveHarness()` and `resolveNativeAgentProvider()` — bypasses the routing layer entirely for the embedded-agent harness. | `cli/internal/agent/runner.go:382`; `cli/internal/agent/agent_runner.go:519` | [ddx-2f5a2284](#) — ModelRef/Profile paths must consult discovery for `--provider` + catalog override |\n| 3 | `FuzzyMatchModel` does no case-folding and no canonical-prefix normalization. `qwen/qwen3.6` will not match `Qwen3.6-35B-A3B-4bit` because the slash, vendor prefix, and case are treated literally. | `cli/internal/agent/discovery.go:76` | [ddx-0486e601](#) — fuzzy matcher lacks case + canonical-prefix normalization |\n| 4 | `scoreCandidate` has no provider-affinity bonus: when two harnesses can serve the same discovered model, the scorer does not prefer the harness whose provider actually advertised the model. | `cli/internal/agent/routing.go:477` | [ddx-3c5ba7cc](#) — tier escalation must respect `--provider` affinity |\n| 5 | Fuzzy-match tiebreak picks shortest-suffix, not highest-version. `qwen3` against `[qwen3.5-27b, qwen3.6-35b]` picks `qwen3.5`. | `cli/internal/agent/discovery.go:100` | [ddx-0216b966](#) — discovery fuzzy match tiebreak |\n| 6 | No pre-dispatch capability check — context window, tool support, effort, permissions. | `cli/internal/agent/routing.go:396` (`rejectIfNotViable` covers availability only) | [ddx-4817edfd](#) — pre-dispatch capability check |\n\n## Sequence Diagram\n\n```mermaid\nsequenceDiagram\n    actor User\n    participant CLI as cli/cmd/work.go + agent_cmd.go\n    participant Norm as NormalizeRouteRequest<br/>(routing.go:289)\n    participant Disc as DiscoverProviderModels<br/>(discovery.go:124)\n    participant Plan as BuildCandidatePlans<br/>(routing.go:27)\n    participant Fuzzy as FuzzyMatchModel<br/>(discovery.go:66)\n    participant Score as scoreCandidate<br/>(routing.go:477)\n    participant Sel as SelectBestCandidate<br/>(routing.go:556)\n    participant Run as Runner.Run<br/>(runner.go:141)\n    participant RunAgent as Runner.RunAgent<br/>(agent_runner.go:65)\n    participant Resolve as resolveEmbeddedAgentProvider<br/>(agent_runner.go:464)\n    participant AgentLib as agentlib.Run<br/>(agent_runner.go:320)\n    participant HTTP as Provider HTTP endpoint\n\n    User->>CLI: ddx work --local --provider vidar-omlx --model-ref qwen/qwen3.6\n    CLI->>Norm: RouteFlags{...}\n    Note over Norm: --provider consumed only<br/>if it names a registered harness<br/>(GAP #1)\n    Norm-->>CLI: RouteRequest{ModelPin, Profile}\n    CLI->>Disc: DiscoverProviderModels(workDir, timeout)\n    Note over Disc: 30s process-wide cache\n    Disc-->>CLI: DiscoveryResult{Providers[]}\n    CLI->>Plan: BuildCandidatePlans(req, states)\n    loop each harness\n        Plan->>Plan: evaluateCandidate\n        Plan->>Plan: rejectIfNotViable\n    end\n    Plan->>Fuzzy: FuzzyMatchModel (when uncataloged)\n    Fuzzy-->>Plan: resolved model ID\n    Plan->>Score: scoreCandidate per plan\n    Plan-->>Sel: []CandidatePlan (ranked)\n    Sel-->>CLI: best plan\n    CLI->>Run: Runner.Run(RunOptions)\n    Run->>RunAgent: if harnessName == \"agent\"\n    RunAgent->>Resolve: resolveEmbeddedAgentProvider(wd, model, provider, modelRef)\n    Resolve->>Disc: second-pass lookup (cached)\n    Resolve-->>RunAgent: {Provider, BaseURL, APIKey, Model}\n    RunAgent->>AgentLib: agentlib.Run(ctx, Request{...})\n    AgentLib->>HTTP: POST /v1/chat/completions\n    HTTP-->>AgentLib: stream | unexpected EOF\n    AgentLib-->>RunAgent: Result\n    RunAgent-->>User: Result (stdout + session log)\n```\n\n## Layer 1 — CLI flag parse\n\n`ddx work` is a thin alias for `ddx agent execute-loop`. All flags are cloned\nfrom the inner command.\n\n- `cli/cmd/work.go:10` — `newWorkCommand` constructs the alias.\n- `cli/cmd/work.go:40` — flags are cloned verbatim from `newAgentExecuteLoopCommand`.\n- `cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:1319` — `--harness` flag declaration.\n- `cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:1320` — `--model` flag declaration.\n- `cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:1321` — `--provider` flag declaration.\n- `cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:1322` — `--model-ref` flag declaration.\n- `cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:1327` — `--local` flag declaration.\n- `cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:1338` — `runAgentExecuteLoop` entry point reads flags.\n- `cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:1342` through `cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:1357` — flag values\n  are plucked into local variables (`harness`, `model`, `provider`, `modelRef`,\n  `effort`, `local`).\n- `cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:1374` — `--local` branch takes the inline path; without\n  it the loop submits to the running DDx server.\n- `cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:1384` — `ValidateForExecuteLoop(harness, model, provider, modelRef)`\n  runs before any bead is claimed.\n\nThe flag values eventually reach `ExecuteBead`, which in turn builds\n`RunOptions`:\n\n- `cli/internal/agent/types.go:62` — `RunOptions` struct definition.\n- `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go:498` — `runner.Run(RunOptions{...})`\n  constructs the options, including `Provider: opts.Provider` on line 504.\n\n**Observation:** `RouteFlags` (`cli/internal/agent/types.go:51`) has a `Provider` field; so does\n`RunOptions` (`cli/internal/agent/types.go:74`). They are the raw CLI inputs. The gap is not here\n— it is at the normalization boundary (Layer 2).\n\n## Layer 2 — Normalize flags into `RouteRequest`\n\nThe normalization boundary converts raw CLI flags into the minimal routing ask\nthat the routing engine evaluates.\n\n- `cli/internal/agent/routing.go:289` — `NormalizeRouteRequest(flags RouteFlags, cfg Config, catalog *Catalog) RouteRequest`.\n- `cli/internal/agent/routing.go:294` — `RouteRequest{Effort, Permissions}`\n  initial struct. Note the absence of `Provider`.\n- `cli/internal/agent/routing.go:303` — `flags.Harness != \"\"` branch assigns\n  `HarnessOverride` directly.\n- `cli/internal/agent/routing.go:305` — `flags.Provider != \"\"` branch: the\n  provider flag is **only** consulted as a harness-alias lookup. If\n  `--provider vidar-omlx` does not match any entry in `builtinHarnesses`, the\n  value is silently discarded.\n- `cli/internal/agent/routing.go:318` — `flags.Model != \"\"` branch routes the\n  model through `catalog.NormalizeModelRef` to produce either `ModelRef` (known\n  in catalog) or `ModelPin` (exact, bypasses catalog policy).\n- `cli/internal/agent/types.go:337` — `RouteRequest` struct definition. Fields:\n  `Profile`, `ModelRef`, `ModelPin`, `Effort`, `Permissions`, `HarnessOverride`.\n  **No `Provider` field — see Gap #1.**\n\n**Concrete outcome for our invocation:**\n`--provider vidar-omlx` does not name a registered harness, so the provider\nflag is dropped at `cli/internal/agent/routing.go:305`. `--model-ref qwen/qwen3.6` flows through\n`NormalizeModelRef`; since it is not in the shared catalog, it becomes a\n`ModelPin`. The resulting `RouteRequest` carries only\n`{ModelPin: \"qwen/qwen3.6\"}`.\n\n## Layer 3 — Discovery probe\n\n- `cli/internal/agent/discovery.go:117` — `init()` sets the process-wide cache\n  TTL to `30 * time.Second`.\n- `cli/internal/agent/discovery.go:124` — `DiscoverProviderModels(workDir, timeout)` entry.\n- `cli/internal/agent/discovery.go:125` through `cli/internal/agent/discovery.go:130` — cache hit returns the cached result if still fresh.\n- `cli/internal/agent/discovery.go:141` through `cli/internal/agent/discovery.go:155` — probes every configured provider via `providerstatus.Probe`; silently skips unreachable ones (partial failure is tolerated).\n- `cli/internal/agent/discovery.go:162` — cache write under mutex.\n- `cli/internal/agent/discovery.go:170` — `InvalidateDiscoveryCache` is the only\n  way to force a re-probe in tests.\n\nDiscovery is invoked on the routing hot-path only when a `ModelPin` is not in\nthe catalog:\n\n- `cli/internal/agent/routing.go:576` — in `ProbeAndBuildCandidatePlans`, the\n  discovery probe fires when `req.ModelPin != \"\" && req.ModelRef == \"\" && r.Discovery == nil`.\n\nDiscovery is invoked a second time on the dispatch path:\n\n- `cli/internal/agent/agent_runner.go:521` — in `resolveNativeAgentProvider`,\n  `r.Discovery.ProvidersForModel(model)` looks up which providers advertised\n  the requested model.\n\n## Layer 4 — Candidate planning\n\n- `cli/internal/agent/routing.go:27` — `BuildCandidatePlans(req, stateOverride)` entry.\n- `cli/internal/agent/routing.go:33` through `cli/internal/agent/routing.go:46` — iterates every registered harness. `TestOnly` harnesses (script, virtual) are skipped unless `req.HarnessOverride` names them explicitly — this is the guard that prevents `script` from leaking into standard-tier fallback chains (see ddx-869848ec in the comment on line 40).\n- `cli/internal/agent/routing.go:47` — calls `evaluateCandidate` per harness.\n- `cli/internal/agent/routing.go:106` — `evaluateCandidate(name, harness, req, stateOverride)`.\n- `cli/internal/agent/routing.go:396` — `rejectIfNotViable(plan, req)` runs the\n  viability gate. Checks:\n  - `cli/internal/agent/routing.go:399` — `!s.Installed`.\n  - `cli/internal/agent/routing.go:402` — `!s.Reachable`.\n  - `cli/internal/agent/routing.go:405` — `!s.Authenticated`.\n  - `cli/internal/agent/routing.go:408` — quota exhausted.\n  - `cli/internal/agent/routing.go:411` — degraded.\n  - `cli/internal/agent/routing.go:414` — policy violation.\n  - `cli/internal/agent/routing.go:418` — harness override mismatch.\n\nNote: none of these check *capability* — context window, tool support, effort\nlevel support. That is Gap #6.\n\n## Layer 5 — Fuzzy matching\n\n- `cli/internal/agent/discovery.go:66` — `FuzzyMatchModel(input)` entry.\n- `cli/internal/agent/discovery.go:70` — delegates to `fuzzyMatchInPool` using\n  `d.AllModels()` (a flat deduplicated pool across all providers — not per-provider).\n- `cli/internal/agent/discovery.go:76` — `fuzzyMatchInPool(input, pool)`.\n- `cli/internal/agent/discovery.go:78` through `cli/internal/agent/discovery.go:82` — exact-match short-circuit.\n- `cli/internal/agent/discovery.go:89` through `cli/internal/agent/discovery.go:96` — prefix match: candidate `m` is considered if `len(m) > len(input) && m[:len(input)] == input`. **The comparison is case-sensitive and treats slashes literally (Gap #3).**\n- `cli/internal/agent/discovery.go:100` through `cli/internal/agent/discovery.go:106` — tiebreak: shortest suffix wins; alphabetical on equal suffix length. **Not version-aware (Gap #5).**\n\n**Concrete outcome for our invocation:**\nPool likely contains `Qwen3.6-35B-A3B-4bit`. Input `qwen/qwen3.6` fails the\nexact check, then fails the prefix check because `Q != q` and the slash is not\nin the pool entries. The function returns `(\"\", false)`. `resolveNativeAgentProvider`\nfalls through to the `isOpenRouterModel` heuristic at\n`cli/internal/agent/agent_runner.go:544` (vendor/model format → openrouter),\nwhich is not what the operator wanted.\n\n## Layer 6 — Scoring\n\n- `cli/internal/agent/routing.go:477` — `scoreCandidate(profile, plan)` entry.\n  Returns a `float64`; When invoked with\n# --strict, also verifies the line contains a Go symbol hint when the path\n# looks like Go source.\n#\n# Cited as the CI step that prevents SD-015-resolution-path-trace.md from\n# rotting silently as routing.go / discovery.go / types.go evolve.\n\nset -euo pipefail\n\nDOC_PATH=\"${1:-docs/helix/02-design/solution-designs/SD-015-resolution-path-trace.md}\"\n\nif [ ! -f \"$DOC_PATH\" ]; then\n    echo \"citations: $DOC_PATH not found\" >&2\n    exit 1\nfi\n\n# Match citations of the form `path/to/file.ext:NN` inside backticks.\n# We only validate citations whose path looks like a file (contains a slash\n# and ends with a known extension).\ncitations=$(grep -oE '`[A-Za-z0-9_./-]+\\.(go|ts|tsx|js|yaml|yml|md|sh):[0-9]+`' \"$DOC_PATH\" | tr -d '`' | sort -u)\n\nif [ -z \"$citations\" ]; then\n    echo \"citations: no citations found in $DOC_PATH — fail-closed\" >&2\n    exit 1\nfi\n\nfailures=0\ntotal=0\n\nwhile IFS= read -r cite; do\n    [ -z \"$cite\" ] && continue\n    total=$((total + 1))\n    path=\"${cite%:*}\"\n    line=\"${cite##*:}\"\n    if [ ! -f \"$path\" ]; then\n        echo \"citations: MISSING FILE: $cite\" >&2\n        failures=$((failures + 1))\n        continue\n    fi\n    # Line count check; awk 'END{print NR}' counts lines portably.\n    nlines=$(awk 'END{print NR}' \"$path\")\n    if [ \"$line\" -gt \"$nlines\" ]; then\n        echo \"citations: LINE OUT OF RANGE: $cite (file has $nlines lines)\" >&2\n        failures=$((failures + 1))\n        continue\n    fi\n    # Sanity check: the line must not be blank, since every real citation\n    # points at a struct, func, or statement.\n    content=$(awk -v n=\"$line\" 'NR==n' \"$path\")\n    if [ -z \"$(echo \"$content\" | tr -d '[:space:]')\" ]; then\n        echo \"citations: BLANK LINE: $cite\" >&2\n        failures=$((failures + 1))\n        continue\n    fi\ndone <<< \"$citations\"\n\nif [ \"$failures\" -gt 0 ]; then\n    echo \"citations: $failures/$total failed for $DOC_PATH\" >&2\n    exit 1\nfi\n\necho \"citations: $total/$total ok for $DOC_PATH\"","is_error":false}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"d932d409-7007-4d29-af52-a78f704e35c0","uuid":"465c02eb-3d0f-4e0e-9149-057b595f6ab5","timestamp":"2026-04-18T06:48:07.386Z","tool_use_result":{"stdout":"#!/usr/bin/env bash\n# Verifies every `path:line` citation in SD-015-resolution-path-trace.md\n# points to a file that exists and a line that exists. ("fix(beadform): use design tokens; replace inline CSS-var status with badge class [ddx-e658e2ae]")
on the current base. Verified in cli/internal/server/frontend/src/lib/components/BeadForm.svelte:

  1. inputClass (line 130) uses `text-body-sm` — not `text-sm`.
  2. labelClass (line 131-132) uses `font-label-caps text-label-caps` — not `text-xs`.
  3. Status <select> (lines 174-178) uses `class="{inputClass} {statusBadgeClass(status)}"`,
     where statusBadgeClass returns Tailwind utility classes (`badge-status-open`,
     `badge-status-in-progress`, `badge-status-blocked`, `badge-status-closed`,
     `badge-status-neutral`). No inline `style="background-color: var(--status-…)"`
     remains.
  4. Cancel button (line 269) and Submit button (line 276) both use `text-body-sm`
     — not `text-sm`.

The previous review's BLOCK ("Diff only contains a result.json execution artifact;
no changes to BeadForm.svelte are visible") appears to have inspected only the
execute-bead bundle delta rather than the merged base state. The change landed
in b9b7932d and has not regressed.

No further code changes are warranted. Recommend closing.
